pr.title: add new Makefile setting to limit number of errors printed per file
pr.author: BR-

summary: Added a root Makefile knob, MAX_ERRORS, that passes CodeWarrior's -maxerrors option through CFLAGS. This lets developers run commands such as `make MAX_ERRORS=1` to limit compiler diagnostics per file and avoid console flooding during decompilation work.

key_files.why_it_matters: Introduced the MAX_ERRORS configurable Make variable and wired it into the CFLAGS used for compilation.

what_changed: Added `MAX_ERRORS ?= 0     # 0 = no maximum` near existing top-level Makefile settings such as GENERATE_MAP, NON_MATCHING, and VERBOSE.
Documented intended usage in the PR body: `make MAX_ERRORS=1` shows one error at a time instead of flooding the console.
The default value preserves existing behavior by using `0` as no maximum.
smart_moves: Used a conditional Makefile default assignment (`?=`), so users can override MAX_ERRORS from the command line without editing the Makefile.
Chose a default of `0` with an inline comment explaining that it means no maximum, minimizing surprise for existing builds.
Added the option to the common CFLAGS line so the setting applies consistently to C compilation rather than requiring per-target changes.
decomp_lessons: Small build-system knobs can materially improve iteration speed and usability when working through large numbers of decompilation compile errors.
Prefer command-line-overridable Make variables for developer workflow settings, especially when different contributors may want different diagnostic verbosity.
When adding compiler options for convenience, keep the default behavior equivalent to the previous build unless there is a strong reason to change it.
naming_conventions: Makefile configuration variables are uppercase with underscores, matching existing names such as `GENERATE_MAP`, `NON_MATCHING`, and `VERBOSE`.
The Make variable `MAX_ERRORS` maps directly and readably to the CodeWarrior compiler flag `-maxerrors`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or code-matching tactic was directly changed; this PR supports matching work indirectly by making compile-error output easier to manage.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify Makefile CFLAGS or add more command-line-overridable build knobs.
Search for uses of `MAX_ERRORS` in documentation or developer setup notes.
Check whether CodeWarrior `-maxerrors 0` is consistently accepted across all build configurations.
